title: Specific permissions for specific users
excerpt: We would love to be able to give specific users specific permissions, without having to change the permissions of everyone else. Our executive team only needs to be able to see the requests that they have submitted, but …

posts.author: bmartineau (Brooke Martin)
posts.cookedHtml: We would love to be able to give specific users specific permissions, without having to change the permissions of everyone else. Our executive team only needs to be able to see the requests that they have submitted, but right now we can’t do that without sending them manual updates. Monday.com keeps suggesting that we change the viewer permissions on each board, but we don’t want it to change for everyone, just for some users.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m in agreement here. We have an internal team, guests, viewers, part-time members, and etc. on our monday account. It’s quite complicated.

For the most part, monday’s current permissions are fine. They way I can set board permissions is actually quite useful and granular. However, it would be extremely helpful if I can grant permissions on a per user basis.

On most of our boards, we need to set permissions on a per user bases:

  1. Assign certain users to edit everything
  2. Assign certain users to edit content
  3. Assign certain users to edit only their content but view everything
  4. Assign certain users to just view and comment
  5. Assign certain users to edit and view only their content
  6. Assign certain users to edit everything, but only view what they are assigned.
  7. and etc.

Example Use Case
I have UserA and UserB and UserC. I have boards BoardZ and BoardX and BoardP and BoardMirror. We’ll ignore workspaces and products for now to not complicate anything.

  • UserA will have access to boards BoardZ and BoardX as to be able to edit everything. BoardP to just view or have access for connecting boards and mirrors.
  • UserB will have access to board BoardZ to edit assigned content (while still being able to create own content AND view ALL content [even not assigned]); BoardX to just view; and BoardP to edit everything.
  • User C will have access to board BoardMirror to edit everything/edit content. The problem is that this board contains mirror columns from BoardP, which contains mirrors from BoardZ and BoardX. Therefore, this user needs just VIEW access for BoardP, BoardZ, and BoardX.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Additionally, it would be great if there was a way for mirror columns could be seen WITHOUT requiring the need for certain users to have access to the source board.

We have many boards that have very sensitive information. We could restrict column view access, but that is very time consuming across all our boards and columns.

posts.author: AmberAlys (Amber Cochran)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be beneficial when also trying to update the board and columns. Many times I am trying new connections and people will jump on using that coloumn before its ready. A testing phase would be an option for those that are creating and maintaining the board.
